task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal command (e.g., cat) displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/exhea_mission_spec.txt.

task_completion.description: Configure GMAT force model with Earth, Sun, and Moon point mass gravity
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI Force Model settings window or GMAT script text showing Point Mass gravity enabled for Earth, Sun, and Moon (Luna), with drag and Solar Radiation Pressure (SRP) disabled.

task_completion.description: Run a baseline simulation with RAAN = 0.0 to determine lifetime
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI running a simulation, plotting an orbit, or displaying console output where the spacecraft's altitude drops below 120 km before the end of the 5-year mission.

task_completion.description: Run a simulation with a stable RAAN that survives the full mission
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI running a simulation or plotting an orbit that successfully propagates for the full 1825 days without the perigee dropping below 120 km.

task_completion.description: Save the successful GMAT script to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal (ls command), or GMAT 'Save As' dialog showing the file 'stable_heo_mission.script' located in the '~/GMAT_output/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write the analysis report with the baseline lifetime and stable RAAN
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of '~/GMAT_output/heo_stability_report.txt' containing exactly the lines 'Baseline_Lifetime_Days: <value>' and 'Stable_RAAN_deg: <value>'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used GMAT to model the mission
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GMAT application GUI open or terminal commands executing GMAT scripts. The agent should not just guess the answers or write a Python script to bypass GMAT.

integrity.description: Verify the reported values come from actual simulation runs
integrity.visual_evidence: The values written in the analysis report should logically correspond to the simulation outputs or script modifications seen in earlier GMAT screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create the output files with fabricated data
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a clear progression of configuring the mission in GMAT, running it, and extracting the results, rather than immediately creating the final script and report files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output script path is '/home/ga/GMAT_output/stable_heo_mission.script'. The expected report path is '/home/ga/GMAT_output/heo_stability_report.txt'. The mission duration is exactly 1825 days. The minimum crashing altitude is 120 km. The acceptable tolerance for baseline evaluation is 15 days.
